source: CIS AMAZON WEB SERVICES FOUNDATIONS BENCHMARK V5.0.0
title: Ensure VPC changes are monitored

input_row.source: CIS AMAZON WEB SERVICES FOUNDATIONS BENCHMARK V5.0.0
input_row.title: Ensure VPC changes are monitored
input_row.description: Real-time monitoring of API calls can be achieved by directing CloudTrail Logs to CloudWatch Logs or an external Security Information and Event Management (SIEM) environment, and establishing corresponding metric filters and alarms. It is possible to have more than one VPC within an account; additionally, it is also possible to create a peer connection between two VPCs, enabling network traffic to route between them. It is recommended that a metric filter and alarm be established for changes made to VPCs.
input_row.rationale: CloudWatch is an AWS native service that allows you to observe and monitor resources and applications. CloudTrail logs can also be sent to an external Security Information and Event Management (SIEM) environment for monitoring and alerting. VPCs in AWS are logically isolated virtual networks that can be used to launch AWS resources. Monitoring changes to VPC configurations will help ensure that VPC traffic flow is not negatively impacted. Changes to VPCs can affect network accessibility from the public internet and additionally impact VPC traffic flow to and from the resources launched in the VPC.
input_row.audit: If you are using CloudTrail trails and CloudWatch, perform the following to ensure that there is at least one active multi-region CloudTrail trail with the prescribed metric filters and alarms configured: 1. Identify the log group name that is configured for use with the active multi-region CloudTrail trail: • List all CloudTrail trails: aws cloudtrail describe-trails • Identify multi-region CloudTrail trails: Trails with "IsMultiRegionTrail" set to true • Note the value associated with "Name":<trail-name> • Note the <trail-log-group-name> within the value associated with "CloudWatchLogsLogGroupArn" o Example: arn:aws:logs:<region>:<account-id>:log- group:<trail-log-group-name>:* • Ensure the identified multi-region CloudTrail trail is active: o aws cloudtrail get-trail-status --name <trail-name> ▪ Ensure IsLogging is set to TRUE • Ensure the identified multi-region CloudTrail trail captures all management events: o aws cloudtrail get-event-selectors --trail-name <trail-name> ▪ Ensure there is at least one event selector for a trail with IncludeManagementEvents set to true and ReadWriteType set to All 2. Get a list of all associated metric filters for the <trail-log-group-name> captured in step 1: aws logs describe-metric-filters --log-group-name <trail-log-group- name> 3. Ensure the output from the above command contains the following: "filterPattern": "{ ($.eventName = CreateVpc) || ($.eventName = DeleteVpc) || ($.eventName = ModifyVpcAttribute) || ($.eventName = AcceptVpcPeeringConnection) || ($.eventName = CreateVpcPeeringConnection) || ($.eventName = DeleteVpcPeeringConnection) || ($.eventName = RejectVpcPeeringConnection) || ($.eventName = AttachClassicLinkVpc) || ($.eventName = DetachClassicLinkVpc) || ($.eventName = DisableVpcClassicLink) || ($.eventName = EnableVpcClassicLink) }" 4. Note the <vpc-changes-metric> value associated with the filterPattern from step 3. 5. Get a list of CloudWatch alarms, and filter on the <vpc-changes-metric> captured in step 4: aws cloudwatch describe-alarms --query 'MetricAlarms[?MetricName==<vpc- changes-metric>]' 6. Note the AlarmActions value; this will provide the SNS topic ARN value. 7. Ensure there is at least one active subscriber to the SNS topic: aws sns list-subscriptions-by-topic --topic-arn <sns-topic-arn> • At least one subscription should have "SubscriptionArn" with a valid AWS ARN. o Example of valid "SubscriptionArn": arn:aws:sns:<region>:<account- id>:<sns-topic-name>:<subscription-id>
input_row.remediation: If you are using CloudTrail trails and CloudWatch, perform the following steps to set up the metric filter, alarm, SNS topic, and subscription: 1. Create a metric filter based on the provided filter pattern that checks for VPC changes and uses the <trail-log-group-name> taken from audit step 1: aws logs put-metric-filter --log-group-name <trail-log-group-name> -- filter-name <vpc-changes-metric> --metric-transformations metricName=<vpc-changes- metric>,metricNamespace='CISBenchmark',metricValue=1 --filter-pattern '{ ($.eventName = CreateVpc) || ($.eventName = DeleteVpc) || ($.eventName = ModifyVpcAttribute) || ($.eventName = AcceptVpcPeeringConnection) || ($.eventName = CreateVpcPeeringConnection) || ($.eventName = DeleteVpcPeeringConnection) || ($.eventName = RejectVpcPeeringConnection) || ($.eventName = AttachClassicLinkVpc) || ($.eventName = DetachClassicLinkVpc) || ($.eventName = DisableVpcClassicLink) || ($.eventName = EnableVpcClassicLink) }' Note : You can choose your own metricName and metricNamespace strings. Using the same metricNamespace for all Foundations Benchmark metrics will group them together. 2. Create an SNS topic that the alarm will notify: aws sns create-topic --name <sns-topic-name> Note : You can execute this command once and then reuse the same topic for all monitoring alarms. Note : Capture the TopicArn that is displayed when creating the SNS topic in step 2. 3. Create an SNS subscription for the topic created in step 2: aws sns subscribe --topic-arn <sns-topic-arn> --protocol <sns-protocol> --notification-endpoint <sns-subscription-endpoints> Note : You can execute this command once and then reuse the same subscription for all monitoring alarms. 4. Create an alarm that is associated with the CloudWatch Logs metric filter created in step 1 and the SNS topic created in step 2: aws cloudwatch put-metric-alarm --alarm-name <vpc-changes-alarm> -- metric-name <vpc-changes-metric> --statistic Sum --period 300 -- threshold 1 --comparison-operator GreaterThanOrEqualToThreshold -- evaluation-periods 1 --namespace 'CISBenchmark' --alarm-actions <sns- topic-arn>  References: 1. CCE-79199-6 2. https://docs.aws.amazon.com/awscloudtrail/latest/userguide/receive-cloudtrail- log-files-from-multiple-regions.html 3. https://docs.aws.amazon.com/awscloudtrail/latest/userguide/cloudwatch-alarms- for-cloudtrail.html 4. https://docs.aws.amazon.com/sns/latest/dg/SubscribeTopic.html Additional Information: Configuring a log metric filter and alarm on a multi-region (global) CloudTrail trail: • ensures that activities from all regions (both used and unused) are monitored • ensures that activities on all supported global services are monitored • ensures that all management events across all regions are monitored
input_row.references: 1. CCE-79199-6 2. https://docs.aws.amazon.com/awscloudtrail/latest/userguide/receive-cloudtrail- log-files-from-multiple-regions.html 3. https://docs.aws.amazon.com/awscloudtrail/latest/userguide/cloudwatch-alarms- for-cloudtrail.html 4. https://docs.aws.amazon.com/sns/latest/dg/SubscribeTopic.html Additional Information: Configuring a log metric filter and alarm on a multi-region (global) CloudTrail trail: • ensures that activities from all regions (both used and unused) are monitored • ensures that activities on all supported global services are monitored • ensures that all management events across all regions are monitored

step1_initial_assessment:
**AUDIT APPROACH:** Automated

**APPROACH DECISION:**

This control verifies configurations that can be inspected using AWS services and APIs, particularly focusing on CloudTrail and CloudWatch. All the necessary configurations such as determining CloudTrail status, setting up metric filters, and CloudWatch alarms can be inspected using AWS CLI commands and boto3 SDK calls. The verification process ensures that CloudTrail logs are capturing the necessary events and that metric filters and alarms are configured to monitor changes to VPCs. This aligns perfectly with an automated approach as it involves searching for specific log metrics and alarm configurations.

**PROGRAM NAME:** aws_cloudtrail_vpc_changes_monitoring_enabled

**AUTOMATION DETAILS:**

- **Identify CloudTrail Trails:**
  - Use `boto3.client('cloudtrail').describe_trails()` to list all CloudTrail trails.
  - Check for trails where `IsMultiRegionTrail` is set to `True` to ensure coverage across multiple regions.
  
- **Verify Active CloudTrail Logging:**
  - Use `boto3.client('cloudtrail').get_trail_status(Name=<trail-name>)` to verify that `IsLogging` is `True`.

- **Ensure Management Events are Captured:**
  - Use `boto3.client('cloudtrail').get_event_selectors(TrailName=<trail-name>)` and check if `IncludeManagementEvents` is set to `True` and `ReadWriteType` is `All`.

- **Check Metric Filters for VPC Changes:**
  - Use `boto3.client('logs').describe_metric_filters(logGroupName=<trail-log-group-name>)` to list metric filters.
  - Verify that the `filterPattern` includes actions related to VPC changes like `CreateVpc`, `DeleteVpc`, `ModifyVpcAttribute`, etc.

- **Validate CloudWatch Alarms:**
  - Use `boto3.client('cloudwatch').describe_alarms()` to check for alarms associated with the VPC changes metric.
  
- **Verify SNS Topic Subscriptions:**
  - Use `boto3.client('sns').list_subscriptions_by_topic(TopicArn=<sns-topic-arn>)` to ensure there is at least one active subscription with a valid AWS ARN.

Pass/Fail criteria:
- Pass if all configurations are set according to the specified audit checklist.
- Fail if any configuration is missing or incorrect, like no active logging, missing metric filters, or alarms for VPC changes are not configured, or SNS topic has no subscriptions.

**SOURCE:** CIS AMAZON WEB SERVICES FOUNDATIONS BENCHMARK V5.0.0
step2_review:
**APPROACH VALIDATION:** AGREE  
- The classification of the audit approach as Automated is correct. The tasks described, such as verifying CloudTrail configurations, metric filters, and CloudWatch alarms, can be effectively performed using AWS APIs, boto3, and AWS CLI. These tasks do not require manual intervention and can be automated.

**TECHNICAL ACCURACY:** 8  
- The automation details are mostly technically accurate. The use of boto3 methods like `describe_trails`, `get_trail_status`, `get_event_selectors`, `describe_metric_filters`, and `describe_alarms` are appropriate for the tasks described. However, the program name `aws_cloudtrail_vpc_changes_monitoring_enabled` could be more descriptive to clearly indicate the specific checks being performed, such as including terms like 'metric_filters' or 'alarms'. Additionally, the `list_subscriptions_by_topic` method is correctly used to verify SNS subscriptions.

**IMPROVEMENTS:**  
- The program name could be improved for clarity. A suggestion would be `aws_cloudtrail_vpc_changes_metric_filters_alarms_check` to better describe the specific checks being performed.
- Consider verifying that the CloudWatch log group associated with the CloudTrail is correctly configured to capture the necessary events by using `boto3.client('logs').describe_log_groups()` to ensure the log group exists and is active.
- Ensure that the metric filters are not only present but also correctly configured to trigger on relevant VPC change events. This can be done by checking the `filterPattern` for specific API actions like `CreateVpc`, `DeleteVpc`, `ModifyVpcAttribute`, etc.
- Verify that the CloudWatch alarms are set with appropriate thresholds and actions to notify on VPC changes.
- Consider using AWS Config rules as an additional layer to ensure continuous compliance monitoring for VPC changes.

**CONFIDENCE:** HIGH  
- The assessment is well-structured and aligns with AWS best practices for automated compliance checks using AWS services and APIs. The technical details provided are mostly accurate, and the approach is feasible.

**FINAL RECOMMENDATION:** Modify  
- Modify the program name to be more descriptive of the specific checks being performed.
- Ensure that the metric filters and alarms are not only present but also correctly configured to trigger on relevant VPC change events.
- Consider adding checks for the configuration of the CloudWatch log group and using AWS Config rules for continuous compliance monitoring.
